verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the F1 beta subunit of mitochondrial ATP synthase, a soluble catalytic protein that assembles as part of the F1 core complex within the mitochondrial matrix. The F1 catalytic domain is not membrane-anchored; the membrane-spanning Fo component (F0 subunits) anchors the holoenzyme to the inner mitochondrial membrane. ATP5F1B itself is an intramitochondrial matrix protein with no signal peptide, TM domain, GPI anchor, or other outer-leaflet trafficking signal. It does not reach the outer face of the plasma membrane.
